publications.title: Advanced AI Scaling Framework Version 2
publications.summary: Meta’s Advanced AI Scaling Framework v2 sets out governance, transparency and risk-management practices for advanced AI systems, structured around catastrophic-outcome risk domains including cyber, chemical/biological and loss-of-control risks.

publications.title: Scaling How We Build and Test Our Most Advanced AI
publications.summary: Meta announces its updated Advanced AI Scaling Framework and related safety/preparedness reporting, framing the work as a scaled approach to testing and governing its most advanced AI systems.

controversies.title: Meta AI training on EU/EEA public posts — NOYB GDPR complaints (2024) and Verbraucherzentrale NRW injunction dismissed by OLG Köln (23 May 2025)
controversies.summary: Meta's plan to train its Llama AI models on public posts of adult EU/EEA Facebook and Instagram users (relying on a GDPR legitimate-interest basis, with an opt-out rather than opt-in) drew sustained privacy objections. In June 2024 Meta paused the EU/EEA rollout after 11 complaints by privacy group NOYB (Max Schrems) to the Irish Data Protection Commission and other supervisory authorities. Meta announced a resumption in 2025; consumer body Verbraucherzentrale NRW sought an emergency injunction to stop it. On 23 May 2025 the Higher Regional Court of Cologne (OLG Köln, case 15 U 132/25) dismissed that injunction application, allowing training to proceed from 27 May 2025. The legitimate-interest basis and opt-out model remain contested and subject to ongoing regulatory scrutiny; reported as a decided injunction matter, not a final ruling on GDPR lawfulness.
controversies.rawExcerpt: Das OLG Köln hat den Antrag der Verbraucherzentrale NRW auf Erlass einer einstweiligen Verfügung gegen das KI-Training mit Nutzerdaten von Facebook und Instagram zurückgewiesen.

corroboration.outlet: Taylor Wessing (legal analysis, OLG Köln 23 May 2025)
